sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a history of low back pain that started approximately 2 hours ago after engaging in yard work.
He reports a tightening sensation in his back and pins and needles in his right foot.
He has had similar episodes in the past, with the last episode resolving within a day.
He reports some improvement with a hot shower and sitting.
His partner reports similar pain when the patient is standing.
The patient denies taking any pain medication prior to his visit today.
Past medical history: Not mentioned.
Review of systems: The patient reports tingling sensation in his right leg.
Current medications: None.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient reports pain on flexion, pain to palpation around the L5 region, and normal dorsiflexion.
His pulses are equal in all extremities.
Test Results: The patient had an x-ray of his low back, which was normal.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute low back strain.
Problem list: Acute low back strain; suspected radiculopathy; past low back strain.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg orally twice daily for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretching and exercises.
I instructed the patient to rest his back and follow up with me in two weeks if his symptoms do not improve.
Follow-up: I instructed the patient to follow up with me in two weeks if his symptoms do not improve.
